meaning: n.家长,族长,创办人,最年长者
examples: Mr. Chou can sound more like a Californian management guru than an Asian corporate patriarch.
The president has promised the patriarch that the cathedral will be rebuilt: walls, dome and all.
One, he said, had been written by Abraham, the Jewish patriarch.
But one other demand made by the head of the church, Patriarch Alexy II, may soon be fufilled.
The old patriarch of the family himself came to see what was going on.
